proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `braf_v600e=1` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `braf_v600e=0`.
Cell lines with `egfr_amplification=1` have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines with `egfr_amplification=0`.
Cell lines with `erbb2_amplification=1` have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines with `erbb2_amplification=0`.
Cell lines with `kras_mutation=1` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with `kras_mutation=0`.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test
analyses.result_summary: `braf_v600e=1`: mean dependency_BRAF=-0.8047 (n=5574) vs all other lines mean=-0.2495; difference=-0.5552; Welch t-test p<1e-300.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test
analyses.result_summary: `egfr_amplification=1`: mean dependency_EGFR=-0.6010 (n=3880) vs all other lines mean=-0.2524; difference=-0.3486; Welch t-test p<1e-300.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test
analyses.result_summary: `erbb2_amplification=1`: mean dependency_ERBB2=-0.6856 (n=4890) vs all other lines mean=-0.2535; difference=-0.4321; Welch t-test p<1e-300.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test
analyses.result_summary: `kras_mutation=1`: mean dependency_KRAS=-0.5594 (n=11842) vs all other lines mean=-0.2520; difference=-0.3075; Welch t-test p<1e-300.

iterations.update: Matched oncogene-driver dependencies were strongly supported, motivating lineage-interaction tests to distinguish true subgroup specificity from driver enrichment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The `braf_v600e` effect on `dependency_BRAF` is more negative in `lineage="skin"` than in non-skin lineages.
The `egfr_amplification` effect on `dependency_EGFR` is more negative in `lineage="brain"` than in non-brain lineages.
The `erbb2_amplification` effect on `dependency_ERBB2` is more negative in `lineage` equal to `breast` or `gastric` than in other lineages.
The `kras_mutation` effect on `dependency_KRAS` is more negative in `lineage="pancreatic"` than in non-pancreatic lineages.

analyses.method: OLS interaction model adjusted for growth rate, Cas9 activity, and screen batch
analyses.result_summary: BRAF lineage interaction: OLS term `braf_v600e:I(lineage == "skin")[T.True]` coefficient=-0.01280 on the dependency score scale, p=0.281, model R^2=0.202.
analyses.formula: dependency_BRAF ~ braf_v600e + I(lineage=="skin") + braf_v600e:I(lineage=="skin") + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + C(screen_batch)

analyses.method: OLS interaction model adjusted for growth rate, Cas9 activity, and screen batch
analyses.result_summary: EGFR lineage interaction: OLS term `egfr_amplification:I(lineage == "brain")[T.True]` coefficient=-0.01966 on the dependency score scale, p=0.2, model R^2=0.071.
analyses.formula: dependency_EGFR ~ egfr_amplification + I(lineage=="brain") + egfr_amplification:I(lineage=="brain") + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + C(screen_batch)

analyses.method: OLS interaction model adjusted for growth rate, Cas9 activity, and screen batch
analyses.result_summary: ERBB2 breast/gastric interaction: OLS term `erbb2_amplification:I((lineage == "breast") | (lineage == "gastric"))[T.True]` coefficient=-0.02548 on the dependency score scale, p=0.0273, model R^2=0.121.
analyses.formula: dependency_ERBB2 ~ erbb2_amplification + I((lineage=="breast") | (lineage=="gastric")) + erbb2_amplification:I((lineage=="breast") | (lineage=="gastric")) + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + C(screen_batch)

analyses.method: OLS interaction model adjusted for SMAD4 loss, growth rate, Cas9 activity, and screen batch
analyses.result_summary: KRAS pancreatic interaction: OLS term `kras_mutation:I(lineage == "pancreatic")[T.True]` coefficient=-0.00243 on the dependency score scale, p=0.821, model R^2=0.127.
analyses.formula: dependency_KRAS ~ kras_mutation + I(lineage=="pancreatic") + kras_mutation:I(lineage=="pancreatic") + smad4_loss + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + C(screen_batch)

iterations.update: Most apparent lineage enrichment was explained by the driver feature itself. ERBB2 showed a small but significant additional breast/gastric interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `smad4_loss=1` further increases KRAS dependency among `kras_mutation=1` lines, making `dependency_KRAS` more negative than expected from the `kras_mutation` and `smad4_loss` main effects.
Cell lines with `nf1_loss=1` have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `nf1_loss=0`, after adjusting for growth rate, Cas9 activity, and screen batch.
The `nf1_loss` effect on `dependency_RIT1` is more negative in `lineage="lung"` than in non-lung lineages.

analyses.method: OLS interaction model adjusted for pancreatic lineage, growth rate, Cas9 activity, and screen batch
analyses.result_summary: KRAS SMAD4 interaction: OLS term `kras_mutation:smad4_loss` coefficient=-0.00016 on the dependency score scale, p=0.987, model R^2=0.127.
analyses.formula: dependency_KRAS ~ kras_mutation + smad4_loss + kras_mutation:smad4_loss + I(lineage=="pancreatic") + C(screen_batch) + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score

analyses.method: OLS main-effect model adjusted for growth rate, Cas9 activity, and screen batch
analyses.result_summary: RIT1 NF1 main effect: OLS term `nf1_loss` coefficient=-0.09540 on the dependency score scale, p=7.32e-69, model R^2=0.009.

analyses.method: OLS interaction model adjusted for growth rate, Cas9 activity, and screen batch
analyses.result_summary: RIT1 NF1 lung interaction: OLS term `nf1_loss:I(lineage == "lung")[T.True]` coefficient=0.00583 on the dependency score scale, p=0.688, model R^2=0.009.
analyses.formula: dependency_RIT1 ~ nf1_loss + I(lineage=="lung") + nf1_loss:I(lineage=="lung") + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + C(screen_batch)

iterations.update: The RIT1 dependency signal tracked NF1 loss broadly rather than being lung-specific. SMAD4 did not modify the KRAS mutation dependency.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `lineage="colorectal"` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.
Cell lines with `apc_mutation=1` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines with `apc_mutation=0`.
Cell lines with `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines below that threshold.
Cell lines with `rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm >= 3.559` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines below that threshold.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test
analyses.result_summary: `lineage="colorectal"`: mean dependency_KIF18A=-0.7204 (n=5816) vs all other lines mean=-0.2524; difference=-0.4680; Welch t-test p<1e-300.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test
analyses.result_summary: `apc_mutation=1`: mean dependency_KIF18A=-0.5447 (n=9285) vs all other lines mean=-0.2526; difference=-0.2921; Welch t-test p<1e-300.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test
analyses.result_summary: `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819`: mean dependency_KIF18A=-0.4694 (n=12508) vs all other lines mean=-0.2526; difference=-0.2168; Welch t-test p<1e-300.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test
analyses.result_summary: `rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm >= 3.559`: mean dependency_KIF18A=-0.3880 (n=12510) vs all other lines mean=-0.2797; difference=-0.1083; Welch t-test p=1.73e-104.

iterations.update: KIF18A dependency was far stronger in the colorectal/APC/WNT axis than in any initial oncogene-addiction screen.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `wnt_activity_score` makes `dependency_KIF18A` more negative much more strongly in `lineage="colorectal"` lines than in non-colorectal lines.

Among cell lines with `lineage="colorectal"`, `apc_mutation=1`, and `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819`, `smad4_loss=1` suppresses KIF18A dependency, producing less negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than `smad4_loss=0`.
Cell lines with `lineage="colorectal"`, `apc_mutation=1`, `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819`, and `smad4_loss=0` have much more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.

analyses.method: OLS interaction model adjusted for APC mutation, SMAD4 loss, growth rate, Cas9 activity, and screen batch
analyses.result_summary: KIF18A WNT by colorectal interaction: OLS term `wnt_activity_score:I(lineage == "colorectal")[T.True]` coefficient=-0.35523 on the dependency score scale, p<1e-300, model R^2=0.203.
analyses.formula: dependency_KIF18A ~ wnt_activity_score + I(lineage=="colorectal") + wnt_activity_score:I(lineage=="colorectal") + apc_mutation + smad4_loss + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + C(screen_batch)

analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test inside the colorectal APC-mutant high-WNT subgroup
analyses.result_summary: `smad4_loss=1` within colorectal APC-mutant high-WNT lines: mean dependency_KIF18A=-0.2557 (n=562) vs `smad4_loss=0` within colorectal APC-mutant high-WNT lines mean=-1.2990 (n=2571); difference=1.0433; Welch t-test p<1e-300.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test
analyses.result_summary: `lineage="colorectal" & apc_mutation=1 & wnt_activity_score >= 0.819 & smad4_loss=0`: mean dependency_KIF18A=-1.2990 (n=2571) vs all other lines mean=-0.2530; difference=-1.0460; Welch t-test p<1e-300.

iterations.update: The complete KIF18A subgroup required both APC/WNT activation and SMAD4 intact status; SMAD4 loss nearly eliminated the dependency inside the same colorectal APC high-WNT background.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `myc_amplification=1` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than cell lines with `myc_amplification=0`.
`copy_number_8q24` is associated with more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores after adjustment for `myc_amplification`, growth rate, Cas9 activity, and screen batch.
Cell lines with both `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` and `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm >= 5.669` have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than all other cell lines.
`myc_amplification=1` independently makes `dependency_DHX9` more negative after controlling for `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm`, growth rate, Cas9 activity, and screen batch.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test
analyses.result_summary: `myc_amplification=1`: mean dependency_RPL11=-0.3614 (n=8055) vs all other lines mean=-0.2523; difference=-0.1091; Welch t-test p=5.71e-140.

analyses.method: OLS model adjusted for MYC amplification, 8q24-by-MYC-amplification interaction, growth rate, Cas9 activity, and screen batch
analyses.result_summary: RPL11 8q24 copy-number model: OLS term `copy_number_8q24` coefficient=-0.10298 on the dependency score scale, p=7.80e-99, model R^2=0.027.
analyses.formula: dependency_RPL11 ~ copy_number_8q24 + myc_amplification + copy_number_8q24:myc_amplification + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + C(screen_batch)

analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test
analyses.result_summary: `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964 & rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm >= 5.669`: mean dependency_DHX9=-0.3892 (n=3320) vs all other lines mean=-0.3297; difference=-0.0595; Welch t-test p=2.48e-21.

analyses.method: OLS model adjusted for MYC expression, MYC-expression-by-amplification interaction, growth rate, Cas9 activity, and screen batch
analyses.result_summary: DHX9 MYC-amplification adjusted model: OLS term `myc_amplification` coefficient=-0.03313 on the dependency score scale, p=0.174, model R^2=0.007.
analyses.formula: dependency_DHX9 ~ rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm + myc_amplification + rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm:myc_amplification + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + C(screen_batch)

iterations.update: RPL11 tracked MYC/8q24 biology. DHX9 was linked to MYC expression and growth, while MYC amplification did not add clear independent DHX9 signal after expression was included.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than lower-growth cell lines.
Cell lines with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than lower-growth cell lines.
`rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` amplifies the growth-rate effect on `dependency_POLR2A`, making high-MYC fast-growing cells more POLR2A-dependent than expected from growth and MYC main effects.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test
analyses.result_summary: `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`: mean dependency_POLR2A=-0.4972 (n=12577) vs all other lines mean=-0.3928; difference=-0.1043; Welch t-test p=1.01e-179.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test
analyses.result_summary: `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`: mean dependency_TMED10=-0.2816 (n=12577) vs all other lines mean=-0.2447; difference=-0.0369; Welch t-test p=2.52e-24.

analyses.method: OLS interaction model adjusted for Cas9 activity and screen batch
analyses.result_summary: POLR2A growth by MYC-expression interaction: OLS term `growth_rate_doublings_per_day:rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` coefficient=-0.00752 on the dependency score scale, p=0.304, model R^2=0.031.

iterations.update: POLR2A and TMED10 behaved mainly as growth-associated broad dependencies; a POLR2A growth-by-MYC expression interaction was not supported.

iterations.screen_description: Systematic heterogeneity screen across `dependency_BRAF`, `dependency_EGFR`, `dependency_ERBB2`, `dependency_KRAS`, and `dependency_RIT1`. Candidate predicates included both states of each binary feature, categorical lineage/subtype/culture/batch levels with at least 500 rows, high/low quartiles for continuous features, and pair/triple combinations with at least 500 rows.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a systematic subgroup search, `braf_v600e=1` defines a subgroup with more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than all other cell lines.
In a systematic subgroup search, `egfr_amplification=1` defines a subgroup with more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than all other cell lines.
In a systematic subgroup search, `erbb2_amplification=1` defines a subgroup with more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than all other cell lines.
In a systematic subgroup search, `kras_mutation=1` defines a subgroup with more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than all other cell lines.
In a systematic subgroup search, `nf1_loss=1` defines a subgroup with more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than all other cell lines.

analyses.method: Systematic single/pair/triple subgroup screen followed by Welch t-test for selected subgroup
analyses.result_summary: `braf_v600e=1`: mean dependency_BRAF=-0.8047 (n=5574) vs all other lines mean=-0.2495; difference=-0.5552; Welch t-test p<1e-300.

analyses.method: Systematic single/pair/triple subgroup screen followed by Welch t-test for selected subgroup
analyses.result_summary: `egfr_amplification=1`: mean dependency_EGFR=-0.6010 (n=3880) vs all other lines mean=-0.2524; difference=-0.3486; Welch t-test p<1e-300.

analyses.method: Systematic single/pair/triple subgroup screen followed by Welch t-test for selected subgroup
analyses.result_summary: `erbb2_amplification=1`: mean dependency_ERBB2=-0.6856 (n=4890) vs all other lines mean=-0.2535; difference=-0.4321; Welch t-test p<1e-300.

analyses.method: Systematic single/pair/triple subgroup screen followed by Welch t-test for selected subgroup
analyses.result_summary: `kras_mutation=1`: mean dependency_KRAS=-0.5594 (n=11842) vs all other lines mean=-0.2520; difference=-0.3075; Welch t-test p<1e-300.

analyses.method: Systematic single/pair/triple subgroup screen followed by Welch t-test for selected subgroup
analyses.result_summary: `nf1_loss=1`: mean dependency_RIT1=-0.3527 (n=4560) vs all other lines mean=-0.2572; difference=-0.0955; Welch t-test p=1.55e-68.

iterations.update: The systematic screen recapitulated the matched driver features for BRAF, EGFR, ERBB2, KRAS, and RIT1 rather than finding stronger complete multi-feature subgroup definitions.

iterations.screen_description: Systematic heterogeneity screen across `dependency_KIF18A`, `dependency_TMED10`, `dependency_DHX9`, `dependency_POLR2A`, and `dependency_RPL11`, using the same single/pair/triple predicate procedure and then refining biologically coherent complete subgroup definitions.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For `dependency_KIF18A`, the complete subgroup `lineage="colorectal"`, `apc_mutation=1`, `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819`, and `smad4_loss=0` has more negative scores than all other cell lines.
For `dependency_TMED10`, the discovered subgroup `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`, `nf1_loss=1`, and `stk11_loss=0` has more negative scores than all other cell lines.
For `dependency_DHX9`, the discovered subgroup `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` and `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm >= 5.669` has more negative scores than all other cell lines.
For `dependency_POLR2A`, the discovered subgroup `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`, `lineage="lung"`, and `copy_number_8q24 >= 0.428` has more negative scores than all other cell lines.

For `dependency_RPL11`, the discovered subgroup `myc_amplification=1`, `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`, `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm >= 5.669`, and `copy_number_8q24 >= 0.428` has more negative scores than all other cell lines.

analyses.method: Systematic subgroup screen plus refined Welch t-test
analyses.result_summary: `lineage="colorectal" & apc_mutation=1 & wnt_activity_score >= 0.819 & smad4_loss=0`: mean dependency_KIF18A=-1.2990 (n=2571) vs all other lines mean=-0.2530; difference=-1.0460; Welch t-test p<1e-300.

analyses.method: Systematic subgroup screen plus refined Welch t-test
analyses.result_summary: `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964 & nf1_loss=1 & stk11_loss=0`: mean dependency_TMED10=-0.3000 (n=1053) vs all other lines mean=-0.2530; difference=-0.0470; Welch t-test p=9.98e-06.

analyses.method: Systematic subgroup screen plus refined Welch t-test
analyses.result_summary: `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964 & rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm >= 5.669`: mean dependency_DHX9=-0.3892 (n=3320) vs all other lines mean=-0.3297; difference=-0.0595; Welch t-test p=2.48e-21.

analyses.method: Systematic subgroup screen plus refined Welch t-test
analyses.result_summary: `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964 & lineage="lung" & copy_number_8q24 >= 0.428`: mean dependency_POLR2A=-0.5361 (n=554) vs all other lines mean=-0.4178; difference=-0.1184; Welch t-test p=2.47e-16.

analyses.method: Systematic subgroup screen plus refined Welch t-test
analyses.result_summary: `myc_amplification=1 & growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964 & rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm >= 5.669 & copy_number_8q24 >= 0.428`: mean dependency_RPL11=-0.4042 (n=1349) vs all other lines mean=-0.2662; difference=-0.1380; Welch t-test p=1.22e-43.

iterations.update: The second systematic screen found one very strong biology-specific subgroup for KIF18A and weaker growth/MYC-associated heterogeneity for broad-essential dependencies.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For `dependency_BRAF`, `braf_v600e=1` remains a strong negative predictor after adjusting for `lineage="skin"`, the BRAF-by-skin interaction, growth rate, Cas9 activity, and screen batch.

For `dependency_EGFR`, `egfr_amplification=1` remains a strong negative predictor after adjusting for `lineage="brain"`, the EGFR-amplification-by-brain interaction, growth rate, Cas9 activity, and screen batch.
For `dependency_KRAS`, `kras_mutation=1` remains a strong negative predictor after adjusting for pancreatic lineage, `smad4_loss`, growth rate, Cas9 activity, and screen batch.
For `dependency_KIF18A`, adding `rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm >= 3.559` to the colorectal APC-mutant high-WNT SMAD4-intact subgroup preserves a very negative subgroup effect.
For `dependency_RPL11`, `myc_amplification=1` and high 8q24/MYC-growth context define a stronger subgroup than `myc_amplification=1` alone.

analyses.method: OLS adjusted main-effect model
analyses.result_summary: Final BRAF adjusted driver model: OLS term `braf_v600e` coefficient=-0.55093 on the dependency score scale, p<1e-300, model R^2=0.202.
analyses.formula: dependency_BRAF ~ braf_v600e + I(lineage=="skin") + braf_v600e:I(lineage=="skin") + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + C(screen_batch)

analyses.method: OLS adjusted main-effect model
analyses.result_summary: Final EGFR adjusted driver model: OLS term `egfr_amplification` coefficient=-0.34520 on the dependency score scale, p<1e-300, model R^2=0.071.
analyses.formula: dependency_EGFR ~ egfr_amplification + I(lineage=="brain") + egfr_amplification:I(lineage=="brain") + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + C(screen_batch)

analyses.method: OLS adjusted main-effect model
analyses.result_summary: Final KRAS adjusted driver model: OLS term `kras_mutation` coefficient=-0.30724 on the dependency score scale, p<1e-300, model R^2=0.127.
analyses.formula: dependency_KRAS ~ kras_mutation + I(lineage=="pancreatic") + kras_mutation:I(lineage=="pancreatic") + smad4_loss + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + C(screen_batch)

analyses.method: Refined subgroup Welch t-test
analyses.result_summary: `lineage="colorectal" & apc_mutation=1 & wnt_activity_score >= 0.819 & smad4_loss=0 & rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm >= 3.559`: mean dependency_KIF18A=-1.3320 (n=1511) vs all other lines mean=-0.2749; difference=-1.0572; Welch t-test p<1e-300.

analyses.method: Refined subgroup Welch t-test
analyses.result_summary: `myc_amplification=1 & growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964 & rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm >= 5.669 & copy_number_8q24 >= 0.428`: mean dependency_RPL11=-0.4042 (n=1349) vs all other lines mean=-0.2662; difference=-0.1380; Welch t-test p=1.22e-43.

iterations.update: Final checks confirmed the matched driver effects remain robust after simple covariate adjustment and that KIF18A/RPL11 have the clearest complete subgroup definitions.

analysis_notes.dependency_score_direction: More negative values indicate stronger dependency after knockout.
